schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: THE SMART READER of DiagnosticBase's dumb breadcrumbs. Two halves. THE REPORT: assemble(records, pointer) crawls the emission breadcrumbs for one pointer, orders them by the 6th-decimal microsecond stamp, and trims them into a coherent first-pass slice a resolver reads WITHOUT a second run — other pointers never bleed in (Law 6), every emitted value carried, the source never mutated (deep-copied). THE LEARNING-LOOP: CompletenessRegistry learns, per transition-class (gate), the value-keys a report must carry. record_miss(gate, key) folds a discovered miss forward; assemble consults the registry and surfaces any learned-required-but-absent key as a LOUD recurrence. Mailbox is a minimal receiver (the receive_diagnostic contract) so an emitter -> mailbox -> interpreter chain runs end to end.
why: Instantiates the concept-piece I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass — a report that resolves the issue on the FIRST pass, a method Akien has proven over decades (2007 test framework, UU, Trouble Tickets; his first-reach debugging tool; half-day->10min). Proven infra built AHEAD of need, not grown-against it. DiagnosticBase.emit left the intelligence OUT of the breadcrumb deliberately (Law 1 — the diagnostic surface's inference compiled ONCE, here, the inference_domain move); this is that one place. The learning-loop makes the report self-teaching (I-learns-its-gates pointed at the diagnostic surface): completeness rises monotonically and no learned gap recurs.
invoke: In code: from cairn.diagnostic_interpreter import assemble, CompletenessRegistry, Mailbox. Wire a Mailbox as a DiagnosticBase receiver (dev.set_diagnostic_receiver(box)); after emissions, report = assemble(box.records(), pointer, registry=reg). Fold a miss: reg.record_miss(gate, key) ('learned' first time, 'recurred' after). Persist: reg.save(path) / CompletenessRegistry.load(path). Prove: python3 cairn/diagnostic_interpreter/proofs/test_interpreter.py (exit 0 = green).
node_class: code-seam — children a (the report/crawler) + b (the learning-loop) of ticket diagnostic-interpreter, built together (Akien scoped report + learning-loop). Child c (the `cairn diagnose <pointer>` CLI face, via cairn-command) is buildable but not yet built — lowest priority, the engine is the substance. Berths in cairn/ (class-space); crawls the breadcrumbs DiagnosticBase.emit sends to CC's mailbox.
how_it_learns: YES — learning is its WHOLE SECOND HALF, not an add-on. The CompletenessRegistry IS a learns-its-gates instance whose endpoint is the diagnostic report: its GATE is a transition-class, its EVIDENCE is a resolver hitting a gap the report should have carried, and its CONFIDENCE-MOVE is folding that key into what the class's report must include (record_miss). The special-class falsifier is the learning rule made physics: a FIRST miss is a feed-forward signal ('learned'); an already-learned key absent from a later report is a RECURRENCE, surfaced LOUD (assemble's completeness.recurrences, complete=False) — the terminal falsifier, a Law 1 defect it refuses to let pass silently. Distinct endpoint from the decision-autonomy store in CairnCommons/learning/ (which learns whether CC may ACT); kin, cross-referenced, NOT merged — a distinct store with a distinct key. Its horizon: where learned completeness durably lives (a commons git-JSON store vs CC's shim when shims land) — injected path today, real home a filed edge.
traces_to: I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass (the concept-piece this instantiates — the report delivers all resolve-data on the first pass)
Law 7 SHARPENED (the diagnostic surface is not merely loud but COMPLETE on first emission; a recurrence is surfaced LOUD, never silent)
Law 1 (re-running to gather more failure data is re-deriving the settled — the waste the complete report kills; and the interpreter's inference is compiled ONCE here)
I-learns-its-gates (the learning-loop is gate-learning pointed at the diagnostic surface — a distinct endpoint: what a report must CARRY)
Law 6 (the emission stays a thin pointer; the interpreter reads and never copies the ticket nor mutates its source records)
cairn/base/diagnostic.py (DiagnosticBase.emit — the breadcrumb this crawls; its docstring named this interpreter as 'the next build')
owner: CC owns the diagnostic_interpreter code + its proof and gates writes to them. The interpreter READS breadcrumbs and logs; it never emits and never owns the tickets it points at (Law 6 — owned data stays with its owner; only the pointer crosses, and the interpreter follows it, it does not copy).
proof: proofs/test_interpreter.py — seven teeth a hollow interpreter could not pass: the report gathers ONE pointer's transaction ordered by the 6th-decimal stamp and isolated (real DiagnosticBase emissions, other pointers excluded — Law 6); it reads without mutating (deep-copied slice — this tooth caught a real shallow-copy defect on first run and drove the fix); a first miss is 'learned' and folded forward so the next report requires it (Law 1); a learned gap that recurs is complete=False and named in recurrences (LOUD, Law 7); a complete report is clean; record_miss distinguishes 'learned' from 'recurred'; learned completeness save->load round-trips (a missing file is an empty registry, not an error). Green bare (python3) and sealed under the tester; VALIDATION persisted beside-code at validations/test_interpreter.json.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the report is INCOMPLETE by construction — it drops an emitted value or bleeds in another pointer's records (not a faithful, isolated slice); (2) the learning-loop records a miss but never changes the next report (learning theater — the fold is a no-op); (3) an already-learned gap recurs and the report stays SILENT about it (the terminal falsifier of the principle — must be loud, Law 7); (4) the interpreter grows an emit path (it becomes a second emitter, collapsing the dumb-breadcrumb / smart-reader split — Law 6); (5) it mutates its source records (it must read, never write, what it is handed); (6) the learned-completeness store is merged into the decision-autonomy gate-store (two distinct learnings collapsed — the recorded distinction).
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) v0 reads the in-memory Mailbox; the 30-day json log tier (instance-space, cache-class) is read only when a real crawl demands it
(b) the durable home for learned completeness (a commons git-JSON store vs CC's shim when shims land) is a filed edge — the store path is injected, proven against a temp
(c) the `cairn diagnose <pointer>` CLI face (child c) is not yet built — buildable via cairn-command, lowest priority
(d) the concept-piece it instantiates is CAST but awaiting Akien's signature gate
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_interpreter.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND the VALIDATION is persisted beside-code (cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation -> validations/test_interpreter.json, done) AND committed (git).
provenance: Born 2026-07-23, built as children a+b of the diagnostic-interpreter ticket cast the same session, which instantiates the concept-piece I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass. The chain: Akien picked 'solidify the layer' (the system_rackmount flake) over the librarian spine; CC measured the flake already fixed + solid and the whole runtime layer's seals current; the open piece was this interpreter, which CC wrongly deferred as speculative; Akien corrected (a decades-proven method, its why uncaptured) and refined the falsifier into the special learning-signal class; CC captured the why and Akien said 'sorted it, build report + learning-loop'. The shallow-copy defect surfaced on the first proof run (the read-never-mutate tooth) and was fixed to a deep copy before the seal — a measured finding, Law 3.
